name: Johns Hopkins SLICE early-Lyme cohort (Aucott/Rebman)
aliases: Study of Lyme Immunology and Clinical Events (SLICE)
denominator_method: Patients with physician-diagnosed early Lyme disease (erythema migrans), ideally treated, enrolled at diagnosis; a group of endemic-area participants without Lyme disease serves as the comparison.
time_zero_definition: Diagnosis and antibiotic treatment of early Lyme disease
notes: Prospective inception cohort of ideally-treated early Lyme disease with a non-Lyme comparison group and a bio-repository, designed specifically to estimate the incidence of post-treatment Lyme disease. Functionally-impairing persistent symptoms occurred in 14% of treated patients vs 4% of controls, giving a rigorous denominator for PTLD that most Lyme literature lacks.

publications.title: Risk of post-treatment Lyme disease in patients with ideally-treated early Lyme disease: a prospective cohort study
publications.authors: Aucott JN, Yang T, Yoon I, Powell D, Geller SA, Rebman AW.

findings.outcome_verbatim: functionally-impairing persistent symptoms (post-treatment Lyme disease) after ideal treatment of early Lyme disease vs non-Lyme controls
findings.source_locator: Results (14% of treated Lyme patients vs 4% of controls)
